objective: Root-cause and fix (if proxy-side) why Cursor-routed models cannot use the Browser plugin (client MCP tool mcp__node_repl__js / OCX_RESPONSES_TOOL_PROVIDER bridge). WP1 reproduce via cursor/gpt-5.6-luna subagent with debug frames; WP2 conditional fix in src/adapters/cursor/** + tests. devlog 260711_cursor_browser_bridge.

workPhases.title: Reproduce + root-cause Browser-plugin failure through Cursor routing (debug-evidenced)

tasks.title: devlog 000 plan doc (mechanism map + repro design)
cursor/gpt-5.6-luna subagent invokes mcp__node_repl__js (trivial JS) + attempts Browser bootstrap
capture /api/debug/logs frames: streamed toolCall vs mcpArgs(OCX) vs not-advertised
definitive root-cause statement (proxy bridge gap / advertisement gap / model-behavior)

workPhases.title: Conditional fix (only if WP1 shows an opencodex proxy-side gap) + tests

tasks.title: P: diff-level plan (010) if a proxy fix is warranted
C: bun test + tsc + sol C-gate; live re-repro

criteria.scenario: cursor/gpt-5.6-luna subagent invokes the client MCP tool mcp__node_repl__js with a trivial script while provider debug is ON
criteria.expectedEvidence: subagent verbatim report (tool name, SUCCESS/FAILURE, exact result/error) + decisive /api/debug/logs frames identifying the code path (streamed toolCall / mcpArgs OCX_RESPONSES_TOOL_PROVIDER / not advertised)
criteria.capturedEvidence: Cursor subagent (cursor/gpt-5.6-luna) tool inventory = only Cursor-native tools, NO mcp__node_repl__js/tool_search; control sol subagent = full MCP suite incl mcp__node_repl__js. Direct proxy probe: requestContextArgs fired (opencodex injected clientToolDefs) yet model replied "I don't have access to a probe_client_tool in this session" while using native shellStreamArgs. Doc: devlog/260711_cursor_browser_bridge/001_wp1_root_cause.md

criteria.scenario: Definitive root cause of why the Browser plugin fails under Cursor routing
criteria.expectedEvidence: a single grounded root-cause statement classified as proxy-bridge-gap | tool-advertisement-gap | cursor-protocol-limitation | model-behavior, tied to specific frames/code lines, recorded in the devlog unit
criteria.capturedEvidence: Root cause = unregistered/synthetic-provider routing gap: Cursor surfaces routable provider.mcpServers MCP tools via the same RequestContext.tools channel, but the synthetic opencodex-responses (OCX_RESPONSES_TOOL_PROVIDER) provider is not routable, so client tools (mcp__node_repl__js -> Browser plugin) are hidden from the model catalog. Documented deliberate gap (devlog _fin/362 overview). Not a permission. A-gate reviewer (sol/Huygens) near-pass, blocker folded.

criteria.expectedEvidence: either a verified proxy-side fix (bun test + tsc green, live re-repro shows the client tool now works through Cursor) OR a justified no-code-change conclusion (NOOP/NEEDS_HUMAN) with evidence; provider debug turned OFF at close
criteria.capturedEvidence: Disposition = NEEDS_HUMAN (justified no-code-change conclusion). No permission toggle exists; AgentRunRequest.mcpTools already tried and rejected by Cursor parser (phase45 129). Real fix (B-1: routable provider identity for client tools; or B-2: browser bridge as a provider.mcpServers server) is a C4 protocol-boundary feature needing live Cursor experimentation - a user scoping decision. Recorded in 001_wp1_root_cause.md Fix feasibility.
